code_review_issues.issue: No stray debug logs. Uses debugLog() (guarded utility) and log.*() (structured logger) throughout — both are intentional and appropriate.
No stray debug logs. Uses debugLog() only at meaningful decision points (plan metadata, run config, thinking content). Clean.
debugLog() call inside createMemo() on every evaluation (line 27-32) logs hasThinking state on every render cycle. Harmless in production if debugLog is gated, but slightly noisy in dev mode.
No async fix visible here — all async operations use void/await correctly. useWorkflows().importFromFile() is awaited. createCheckpoint().then() uses void. No issues found.

run_agent_from_history() duplicates ~100 lines of the submit_goal() handler (forwarder setup, approval/question/plan forwarding, spawn logic). This is significant code duplication that could introduce bugs if one path is updated but not the other. No compile errors.

All routes registered correctly and match the documented endpoint table. mid-stream messaging routes (steer, follow-up, post-complete, queue, queue/clear) all present. Session CRUD, models, providers, permissions, log, health, and WS all registered.

register_default_tools() registers 9 tools (read, write, edit, bash, glob, grep + web_fetch, web_search, git_read), not 6. The comment in the function says 'Core 6: file I/O + search + shell' but then adds '+3: web + git'. CLAUDE.md states the default set is capped at 6. The 9-tool count conflicts with the documented 6-tool cap. This may be an intentional architectural decision post-doc, but the discrepancy should be noted.

18 vitest tests fail. Root cause: all failures are 'Failed to parse URL from /api/...' — the e2e and integration tests are attempting real HTTP calls against a server that is not running during the test suite. Tests in tests/e2e/real-scenarios.test.ts, tests/e2e/hooks.test.ts, tests/e2e/rust-bridge.test.ts, src/hooks/useChat.integration.test.ts, src/components/chat/ChatView.integration.test.tsx, and src/services/tauri-agent.test.ts all depend on a live Rust backend or Tauri IPC. These are infrastructure-dependent failures, not logic regressions. The 1 additional hooks.test.ts failure (useRustTools loads and executes tools) is an assertion mismatch likely related to the same missing backend.

1 ESLint error: solid/prefer-for — an Array#map is used where SolidJS <For /> component should be used (causes DOM elements to be recreated). 109 ESLint warnings, mostly solid/reactivity warnings about reactive variables used outside tracked scopes. Pre-existing baseline issues, not introduced by recent changes.

summary: Rust backend is clean: cargo check passes, 0 clippy warnings, 1798 tests pass, fmt clean. TypeScript compiles cleanly (tsc pass). Vitest has 18 failures — all are infrastructure-dependent e2e/integration tests that require a live Rust server or Tauri IPC; no unit test regressions. ESLint has 1 error (solid/prefer-for) and 109 warnings (pre-existing). Key findings: (1) register_default_tools() registers 9 tools not 6 as CLAUDE.md documents — intentional expansion or doc drift; (2) run_agent_from_history() in api.rs duplicates ~100 lines of submit_goal() creating maintenance risk; (3) vitest e2e failures are environment issues, not code bugs. No debug logs found in reviewed frontend files.
